arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — RAG-Powered Blazor Chatbot

## Problem

Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions about internal product documentation (PDFs). Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for storage and a local `Docs/` folder for source documents.

## Approach — Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

The chatbot uses a RAG pipeline: PDFs are parsed, chunked, and embedded into Postgres with pgvector. When a user asks a question, the system embeds the query, performs vector similarity search to retrieve relevant chunks, and sends them as context to the LLM to generate a grounded answer with source attribution.

## Technology Decisions

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Real-time streaming chat; single C# stack |
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response (no agents needed) |
| LLM provider | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` | Concrete OpenAI/Azure OpenAI backing for `IChatClient` |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI+OpenAI | Same abstraction layer; `text-embedding-3-small` model |
| Vector store | Postgres + pgvector via `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` | User requested Postgres; no official MEVD pgvector connector exists yet |
| PDF parsing | `PdfPig` | Pure .NET, no native deps, widely used |
| ORM / data access | EF Core + `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | Chat history + vector storage in one DB |
| Styling | Bootstrap 5 (Blazor default) | Simple, clean chat UI |

**Why not `Microsoft.Agents.AI`?** This is a straightforward prompt→response RAG scenario with no tool calling or agentic loops. `IChatClient` is the correct layer per the decision tree.

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   └── Chat.razor              # Chat UI with streaming
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   └── ChatDbContext.cs             # EF Core context (chat history + doc chunks)
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs               # Chat message entity
│   ├── ChatSession.cs               # Session grouping
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs             # Embedded text chunk with vector
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs               # RAG orchestration (embed → search → prompt → stream)
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs  # PDF parsing, chunking, embedding, upsert
│   └── VectorSearchService.cs       # pgvector cosine similarity search
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf            # (existing)
├── Program.cs                       # DI, middleware, config
├── appsettings.json                 # Connection strings, AI config
└── HelpChatbot.csproj               # Package references
```

## NuGet Packages

```xml
<!-- AI / LLM -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="OpenAI" Version="2.*" />

<!-- Postgres + pgvector -->
<PackageReference Include="Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Pgvector" Version="0.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore" Version="0.*" />

<!-- PDF parsing -->
<PackageReference Include="PdfPig" Version="0.*" />

<!-- Blazor (built into SDK — no extra package needed) -->
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Update `HelpChatbot.csproj` with all NuGet packages above
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string, OpenAI settings (model IDs, docs path)
- Set up `Program.cs` with DI registrations for all services

### 2. Data models & EF Core
- Create `DocumentChunk` entity: Id, FileName, PageNumber, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (pgvector `vector` type)
- Create `ChatSession` entity: Id, CreatedAt
- Create `ChatMessage` entity: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Sources (JSON), Timestamp
- Create `ChatDbContext` with pgvector configuration and indexes
- Add EF Core migration

### 3. Document ingestion service
- `DocumentIngestionService`: scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Uses PdfPig to extract text page-by-page
- Chunks text by paragraphs (~500 tokens per chunk, with overlap)
- Generates embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upserts chunks + embeddings into Postgres
- Runs on app startup via `IHostedService` (only re-ingests new/changed files)

### 4. Vector search service
- `VectorSearchService`: accepts a query string
- Embeds the query using `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Runs cosine similarity search against `DocumentChunk` using pgvector `<=>` operator
- Returns top-K results above a minimum similarity threshold (0.7)
- Includes source attribution (file name, page number)

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- `ChatService`: accepts user message + session ID
- Calls `VectorSearchService` to retrieve relevant chunks
- Builds a system prompt with retrieved context and instructions (answer only from docs, cite sources)
- Streams LLM response via `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync()`
- Persists user message + assistant response + sources to `ChatMessage` table
- Sets temperature=0, explicit max tokens, model version pinning

### 6. Blazor UI
- Set up Blazor Server with interactive SSR (`AddRazorComponents().AddInteractiveServerComponents()`)
- `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor`, `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- `Chat.razor` page:
  - Chat message list with user/assistant bubbles
  - Input textbox with send button
  - Streaming response display (tokens appear as they arrive)
  - Source citations shown under assistant messages
  - New session button
  - Auto-scroll to latest message

### 7. Configuration & guardrails
- API key loaded from config/env vars (not hardcoded)
- Temperature = 0 for factual responses
- Model version pinned (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- Minimum similarity score threshold on vector search
- System prompt instructs LLM to only answer from provided context
- Graceful error handling if LLM or DB is unavailable

### 8. Build verification
- `dotnet build` passes cleanly
- Verify EF Core migration generates correctly

## Notes

- **Postgres must be running** with the `pgvector` extension installed (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`). The app will attempt to ensure this via EF Core migration.
- **API key**: The user must provide an OpenAI API key via `appsettings.json`, environment variable, or user-secrets. The plan uses `gpt-4o-mini` for chat and `text-embedding-3-small` for embeddings (cost-efficient defaults).
- **No agents needed**: This is a single-turn RAG scenario — `IChatClient` is the correct abstraction. If tool calling or multi-step reasoning is added later, migrate to `Microsoft.Agents.AI`.
- **Ingestion is idempotent**: Re-running the app won't duplicate chunks — it checks by filename hash.

Let me check the Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore package - that's a well-established package that works with EF Core for pgvector. Since there's no official MEVD Postgres connector, I have two options:

1. Use `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` with EF Core directly for vector operations (simpler, more established)
2. Use `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` with a custom Postgres implementation

Given that the user wants Postgres and there's no official MEVD pgvector connector, I'll go with option 1 - using `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` alongside `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` for both regular data and vector search. This is the more practical approach.


Now I'm mapping out the full architecture: a RAG pipeline that ingests PDFs by chunking the text and generating embeddings to store in Postgres with pgvector, then handles queries by embedding the user's question, searching for relevant vectors, and injecting that context into an LLM response. I'll use Blazor Server for the UI, Microsoft.Extensions.AI with OpenAI for the language model, OpenAI's embedding model through MEAI, and EF Core with pgvector for the vector store.

Setting up the project structure with Blazor components for the chat interface, a data layer with EF Core and migrations, and models for storing chat messages and document chunks.

## Approach — Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

The chatbot uses a RAG pipeline: PDFs are parsed, chunked, and embedded into Postgres with pgvector. When a user asks a question, the system embeds the query, performs vector similarity search to retrieve relevant chunks, and sends them as context to the LLM to generate a grounded answer with source attribution.

## Technology Decisions

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Real-time streaming chat; single C# stack |
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response (no agents needed) |
| LLM provider | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` | Concrete OpenAI/Azure OpenAI backing for `IChatClient` |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI+OpenAI | Same abstraction layer; `text-embedding-3-small` model |
| Vector store | Postgres + pgvector via `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` | User requested Postgres; no official MEVD pgvector connector exists yet |
| PDF parsing | `PdfPig` | Pure .NET, no native deps, widely used |
| ORM / data access | EF Core + `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | Chat history + vector storage in one DB |
| Styling | Bootstrap 5 (Blazor default) | Simple, clean chat UI |

**Why not `Microsoft.Agents.AI`?** This is a straightforward prompt→response RAG scenario with no tool calling or agentic loops. `IChatClient` is the correct layer per the decision tree.

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   └── Chat.razor              # Chat UI with streaming
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   └── ChatDbContext.cs             # EF Core context (chat history + doc chunks)
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs               # Chat message entity
│   ├── ChatSession.cs               # Session grouping
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs             # Embedded text chunk with vector
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs               # RAG orchestration (embed → search → prompt → stream)
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs  # PDF parsing, chunking, embedding, upsert
│   └── VectorSearchService.cs       # pgvector cosine similarity search
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf            # (existing)
├── Program.cs                       # DI, middleware, config
├── appsettings.json                 # Connection strings, AI config
└── HelpChatbot.csproj               # Package references
```

## NuGet Packages

```xml
<!-- AI / LLM -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="OpenAI" Version="2.*" />

<!-- Postgres + pgvector -->
<PackageReference Include="Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Pgvector" Version="0.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore" Version="0.*" />

<!-- PDF parsing -->
<PackageReference Include="PdfPig" Version="0.*" />

<!-- Blazor (built into SDK — no extra package needed) -->
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Update `HelpChatbot.csproj` with all NuGet packages above
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string, OpenAI settings (model IDs, docs path)
- Set up `Program.cs` with DI registrations for all services

### 2. Data models & EF Core
- Create `DocumentChunk` entity: Id, FileName, PageNumber, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (pgvector `vector` type)
- Create `ChatSession` entity: Id, CreatedAt
- Create `ChatMessage` entity: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Sources (JSON), Timestamp
- Create `ChatDbContext` with pgvector configuration and indexes
- Add EF Core migration

### 3. Document ingestion service
- `DocumentIngestionService`: scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Uses PdfPig to extract text page-by-page
- Chunks text by paragraphs (~500 tokens per chunk, with overlap)
- Generates embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upserts chunks + embeddings into Postgres
- Runs on app startup via `IHostedService` (only re-ingests new/changed files)

### 4. Vector search service
- `VectorSearchService`: accepts a query string
- Embeds the query using `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Runs cosine similarity search against `DocumentChunk` using pgvector `<=>` operator
- Returns top-K results above a minimum similarity threshold (0.7)
- Includes source attribution (file name, page number)

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- `ChatService`: accepts user message + session ID
- Calls `VectorSearchService` to retrieve relevant chunks
- Builds a system prompt with retrieved context and instructions (answer only from docs, cite sources)
- Streams LLM response via `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync()`
- Persists user message + assistant response + sources to `ChatMessage` table
- Sets temperature=0, explicit max tokens, model version pinning

### 6. Blazor UI
- Set up Blazor Server with interactive SSR (`AddRazorComponents().AddInteractiveServerComponents()`)
- `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor`, `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- `Chat.razor` page:
  - Chat message list with user/assistant bubbles
  - Input textbox with send button
  - Streaming response display (tokens appear as they arrive)
  - Source citations shown under assistant messages
  - New session button
  - Auto-scroll to latest message

### 7. Configuration & guardrails
- API key loaded from config/env vars (not hardcoded)
- Temperature = 0 for factual responses
- Model version pinned (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- Minimum similarity score threshold on vector search
- System prompt instructs LLM to only answer from provided context
- Graceful error handling if LLM or DB is unavailable

### 8. Build verification
- `dotnet build` passes cleanly
- Verify EF Core migration generates correctly

## Notes

- **Postgres must be running** with the `pgvector` extension installed (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`). The app will attempt to ensure this via EF Core migration.
- **API key**: The user must provide an OpenAI API key via `appsettings.json`, environment variable, or user-secrets. The plan uses `gpt-4o-mini` for chat and `text-embedding-3-small` for embeddings (cost-efficient defaults).
- **No agents needed**: This is a single-turn RAG scenario — `IChatClient` is the correct abstraction. If tool calling or multi-step reasoning is added later, migrate to `Microsoft.Agents.AI`.
- **Ingestion is idempotent**: Re-running the app won't duplicate chunks — it checks by filename hash.

## Problem

Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions about internal product documentation (PDFs). Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for storage and a local `Docs/` folder for source documents.

## Approach — Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

The chatbot uses a RAG pipeline: PDFs are parsed, chunked, and embedded into Postgres with pgvector. When a user asks a question, the system embeds the query, performs vector similarity search to retrieve relevant chunks, and sends them as context to the LLM to generate a grounded answer with source attribution.

## Technology Decisions

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Real-time streaming chat; single C# stack |
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response (no agents needed) |
| LLM provider | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` | Concrete OpenAI/Azure OpenAI backing for `IChatClient` |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI+OpenAI | Same abstraction layer; `text-embedding-3-small` model |
| Vector store | Postgres + pgvector via `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` | User requested Postgres; no official MEVD pgvector connector exists yet |
| PDF parsing | `PdfPig` | Pure .NET, no native deps, widely used |
| ORM / data access | EF Core + `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | Chat history + vector storage in one DB |
| Styling | Bootstrap 5 (Blazor default) | Simple, clean chat UI |

**Why not `Microsoft.Agents.AI`?** This is a straightforward prompt→response RAG scenario with no tool calling or agentic loops. `IChatClient` is the correct layer per the decision tree.

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   └── Chat.razor              # Chat UI with streaming
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   └── ChatDbContext.cs             # EF Core context (chat history + doc chunks)
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs               # Chat message entity
│   ├── ChatSession.cs               # Session grouping
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs             # Embedded text chunk with vector
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs               # RAG orchestration (embed → search → prompt → stream)
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs  # PDF parsing, chunking, embedding, upsert
│   └── VectorSearchService.cs       # pgvector cosine similarity search
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf            # (existing)
├── Program.cs                       # DI, middleware, config
├── appsettings.json                 # Connection strings, AI config
└── HelpChatbot.csproj               # Package references
```

## NuGet Packages

```xml
<!-- AI / LLM -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="OpenAI" Version="2.*" />

<!-- Postgres + pgvector -->
<PackageReference Include="Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL" Version="10.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Pgvector" Version="0.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore" Version="0.*" />

<!-- PDF parsing -->
<PackageReference Include="PdfPig" Version="0.*" />

<!-- Blazor (built into SDK — no extra package needed) -->
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Update `HelpChatbot.csproj` with all NuGet packages above
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string, OpenAI settings (model IDs, docs path)
- Set up `Program.cs` with DI registrations for all services

### 2. Data models & EF Core
- Create `DocumentChunk` entity: Id, FileName, PageNumber, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (pgvector `vector` type)
- Create `ChatSession` entity: Id, CreatedAt
- Create `ChatMessage` entity: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Sources (JSON), Timestamp
- Create `ChatDbContext` with pgvector configuration and indexes
- Add EF Core migration

### 3. Document ingestion service
- `DocumentIngestionService`: scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Uses PdfPig to extract text page-by-page
- Chunks text by paragraphs (~500 tokens per chunk, with overlap)
- Generates embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upserts chunks + embeddings into Postgres
- Runs on app startup via `IHostedService` (only re-ingests new/changed files)

### 4. Vector search service
- `VectorSearchService`: accepts a query string
- Embeds the query using `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Runs cosine similarity search against `DocumentChunk` using pgvector `<=>` operator
- Returns top-K results above a minimum similarity threshold (0.7)
- Includes source attribution (file name, page number)

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- `ChatService`: accepts user message + session ID
- Calls `VectorSearchService` to retrieve relevant chunks
- Builds a system prompt with retrieved context and instructions (answer only from docs, cite sources)
- Streams LLM response via `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync()`
- Persists user message + assistant response + sources to `ChatMessage` table
- Sets temperature=0, explicit max tokens, model version pinning

### 6. Blazor UI
- Set up Blazor Server with interactive SSR (`AddRazorComponents().AddInteractiveServerComponents()`)
- `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor`, `MainLayout.razor` — standard Blazor scaffolding
- `Chat.razor` page:
  - Chat message list with user/assistant bubbles
  - Input textbox with send button
  - Streaming response display (tokens appear as they arrive)
  - Source citations shown under assistant messages
  - New session button
  - Auto-scroll to latest message

### 7. Configuration & guardrails
- API key loaded from config/env vars (not hardcoded)
- Temperature = 0 for factual responses
- Model version pinned (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- Minimum similarity score threshold on vector search
- System prompt instructs LLM to only answer from provided context
- Graceful error handling if LLM or DB is unavailable

### 8. Build verification
- `dotnet build` passes cleanly
- Verify EF Core migration generates correctly

## Notes

- **Postgres must be running** with the `pgvector` extension installed (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`). The app will attempt to ensure this via EF Core migration.
- **API key**: The user must provide an OpenAI API key via `appsettings.json`, environment variable, or user-secrets. The plan uses `gpt-4o-mini` for chat and `text-embedding-3-small` for embeddings (cost-efficient defaults).
- **No agents needed**: This is a single-turn RAG scenario — `IChatClient` is the correct abstraction. If tool calling or multi-step reasoning is added later, migrate to `Microsoft.Agents.AI`.
- **Ingestion is idempotent**: Re-running the app won't duplicate chunks — it checks by filename hash.

# HelpChatbot — RAG-Powered Blazor Chatbot

## Problem

Build a web-based LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions about internal product documentation (PDFs). Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for storage and a local `Docs/` folder for source documents.

## Approach — Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

The chatbot uses a RAG pipeline: PDFs are parsed, chunked, and embedded into Postgres with pgvector. When a user asks a question, the system embeds the query, performs vector similarity search to retrieve relevant chunks, and sends them as context to the LLM to generate a grounded answer with source attribution.

## Technology Decisions

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Real-time streaming chat; single C# stack |
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; single prompt→response (no agents needed) |
| LLM provider | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` | Concrete OpenAI/Azure OpenAI backing for `IChatClient` |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI+OpenAI | Same abstraction layer; `text-embedding-3-small` model |
| Vector store | Postgres + pgvector via `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` | User requested Postgres; no official MEVD pgvector connector exists yet |
| PDF parsing | `PdfPig` | Pure .NET, no native deps, widely used |
| ORM / data access | EF Core + `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` | Chat history + vector storage in one DB |
| Styling | Bootstrap 5 (Blazor default) | Simple, clean chat UI |

**Why not `Microsoft.Agents.AI`?** This is a straightforward prompt→response RAG scenario with no tool calling or agentic loops. `IChatClient` is the correct layer per the decision tree.

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   └── Chat.razor              # Chat UI with streaming
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   └── ChatDbContext.cs             # EF Core context (chat history + doc chunks)
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs               # Chat message entity
│   ├── ChatSession.cs               # Session grouping
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs             # Embedded text chunk with vector
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs               # RAG orchestration (embed → search → prompt → stream)
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs  # PDF parsing, chunking, embedding, upsert
│   └── VectorSearchService.cs       # pgvector cosine similarity search
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf            # (existing)
├── Program.cs                       # DI, middleware, config
├── appsettings.json                 # Connection strings, AI config
└── HelpChatbot.csproj               # Package references
```

## NuGet Packages

```xml
<!-- AI / LLM -->
```
```xml
Microsoft.Extensions.AI (10.*)
Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI (10.*)
OpenAI (2.*)

<!-- Postgres + pgvector -->
Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL (10.*)
Pgvector (0.*)
Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore (0.*)

<!-- PDF parsing -->
PdfPig (0.*)
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Update `HelpChatbot.csproj` with all NuGet packages
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string, OpenAI settings
- Set up `Program.cs` with DI registrations for all services

### 2. Data models & EF Core
- `DocumentChunk`: Id, FileName, PageNumber, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (vector), ContentHash
- `ChatSession`: Id, CreatedAt
- `ChatMessage`: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Sources (JSON), Timestamp
- `ChatDbContext` with pgvector config and HNSW index on embeddings

### 3. Document ingestion service
- Scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs on startup via `IHostedService`
- PdfPig extracts text page-by-page
- Chunks by paragraphs (~500 tokens, with overlap)
- Embeds via `IEmbeddingGenerator` and upserts into Postgres
- Idempotent — skips already-ingested files (by content hash)

### 4. Vector search service
- Embeds query via `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Cosine similarity search using pgvector `<=>` operator
- Top-5 results above 0.7 similarity threshold
- Returns source attribution (file name, page number)

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- Retrieves relevant chunks → builds system prompt with context
- Streams LLM response via `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync()`
- Persists messages + sources to DB
- Temperature=0, max tokens, pinned model version

### 6. Blazor UI
- Interactive Server-rendered Blazor app
- Chat page with message bubbles, streaming response, source citations
- Input textbox + send button, new session button, auto-scroll

### 7. Configuration & guardrails
- API key from config/env (never hardcoded)
- System prompt constrains answers to provided documentation only
- Graceful error handling for LLM/DB failures

### 8. Build verification
- `dotnet build` passes cleanly

## Notes

- **Postgres** must be running with `pgvector` extension. The DB migration handles `CREATE EXTENSION vector`.
- **API key**: User provides OpenAI key via config, env var, or user-secrets. Defaults: `gpt-4o-mini` (chat), `text-embedding-3-small` (embeddings).
- **No agents needed**: Single-turn RAG — `IChatClient` is correct. Migrate to `Microsoft.Agents.AI` only if tool calling is added later.
- **Ingestion is idempotent**: Checks content hash to avoid re-processing unchanged files.

```
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs               # RAG orchestration (embed → search → prompt → stream)
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs  # PDF parsing, chunking, embedding, upsert
│   └── VectorSearchService.cs       # pgvector cosine similarity search
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf            # (existing)
├── Program.cs                       # DI, middleware, config
├── appsettings.json                 # Connection strings, AI config
└── HelpChatbot.csproj               # Package references
```

## NuGet Packages

```xml
Microsoft.Extensions.AI (10.*)
Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI (10.*)
OpenAI (2.*)
Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL (10.*)
Pgvector (0.*)
Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore (0.*)
PdfPig (0.*)
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Update HelpChatbot.csproj with all NuGet packages
- Configure appsettings.json with Postgres connection string, OpenAI settings
- Set up Program.cs with DI registrations for all services

### 2. Data models & EF Core
- DocumentChunk: Id, FileName, PageNumber, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (vector), ContentHash
- ChatSession: Id, CreatedAt
- ChatMessage: Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Sources (JSON), Timestamp
- ChatDbContext with pgvector config and HNSW index on embeddings

### 3. Document ingestion service
- Scans Docs/ folder for PDFs on startup via IHostedService
- PdfPig extracts text page-by-page
- Chunks by paragraphs (~500 tokens, with overlap)
- Embeds via IEmbeddingGenerator and upserts into Postgres
- Idempotent — skips already-ingested files (by content hash)

### 4. Vector search service
- Embeds query via IEmbeddingGenerator
- Cosine similarity search using pgvector <=> operator
- Top-5 results above 0.7 similarity threshold
- Returns source attribution (file name, page number)

### 5. Chat service (RAG orchestration)
- Retrieves relevant chunks, builds system prompt with context
- Streams LLM response via IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync()
- Persists messages + sources to DB
- Temperature=0, max tokens, pinned model version

### 6. Blazor UI
- Interactive Server-rendered Blazor app
- Chat page with message bubbles, streaming response, source citations
- Input textbox + send button, new session button, auto-scroll

### 7. Configuration & guardrails
- API key from config/env (never hardcoded)
- System prompt constrains answers to provided documentation only
- Graceful error handling for LLM/DB failures

### 8. Build verification
- dotnet build passes cleanly

## Notes

- **Postgres** must be running with pgvector extension. The DB migration handles CREATE EXTENSION vector.
- **API key**: User provides OpenAI key via config, env var, or user-secrets. Defaults: gpt-4o-mini (chat), text-embedding-3-small (embeddings).
- **No agents needed**: Single-turn RAG — IChatClient is correct. Migrate to Microsoft.Agents.AI only if tool calling is added later.
- **Ingestion is idempotent**: Checks content hash to avoid re-processing unchanged files.

- **`PdfPig`** for PDF parsing
- **Blazor Server** for streaming chat UI
- **`gpt-4o-mini`** + **`text-embedding-3-small`** (cost-efficient defaults)

5. Chat service (RAG orchestration with streaming)
